neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO Baroque colonial center with grand aqueduct, plazas, and one of Mexico's best-preserved historic districts
neighborhoods.character: UNESCO Baroque, grand aqueduct, cultural hub

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial apartments and converted mansions

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Querétaro. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Juriquilla
neighborhoods.description: Modern northern suburb with university campus, golf courses, and newer developments

housing.predominantType: Modern houses, gated communities, and apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Querétaro's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: El Marques / Corregidora
neighborhoods.description: Southern municipalities with industrial parks, new housing, and affordable options

housing.predominantType: New construction houses and apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Jurica / Hacienda el Campanario
neighborhoods.description: Upscale northern neighborhoods with hacienda-style living and international school access
neighborhoods.character: Upscale hacienda style, international schools, quiet
neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
